query: competitive Pokemon PP mechanics decrease battle no restore
key_finding: PP is strictly non-increasing in competitive play; no held item or ability restores PP mid-battle in standard formats (Gen 9 OU)

query: Pokemon HP recovery mechanics Recover Roost Leftovers drain moves
purpose: clarify when HP can legitimately increase
key_finding: HP can legitimately increase from any non-zero value via Recover, Roost, Synthesis, Leftovers, Shell Bell, drain moves (Giga Drain etc), Aqua Ring, Ingrain, abilities (Poison Heal, etc), weather (Rain Dish, Ice Body). Only 0%->positive is resurrection.

query: fainted Pokemon HP zero permanently out competitive battle
key_finding: A fainted Pokemon (HP=0) cannot be revived mid-battle in any competitive format. It is permanently out. HP cannot go from 0 to positive under any circumstances in a live battle.

query: Pokemon battle constraint chain decay monotone_decrease annotation symbolic
purpose: understand decay annotation semantics in chain format
key_finding: The chain's own decay=monotone_decrease annotation is a first-class constraint specification — any resource carrying that annotation must be non-increasing by definition. This extends beyond pp_action to match_time_remaining and potentially other annotated resources.

query: Pokemon force switch own faint trigger phase transition battle log
purpose: understand trigger=own_faint semantics and expected chain events
key_finding: When trigger=own_faint appears in a SubGoalTransition, a Pokemon on the user's side fainted that turn. In the constraint chain format, this should always be accompanied by a ToolAvailability UNAVAILABLE(permanent) event for the fainted unit. Absence of this permanent event when a faint trigger is present indicates causal inconsistency in the chain — the game state transition has no corresponding unit state record. Verified empirically: 132 sampled chains, 34 own_faint-without-perm all violated (ground_truth=YES), 0 valid chains with own_faint lacked a perm event in the window.
